A.5.1.notes: Board-approved IS policy published on intranet. Annual review scheduled for December. Policy owner: CISO.

A.5.2.notes: RACI matrix defines CISO, DPO, System Owners, and all-staff roles. Embedded in onboarding pack.

A.5.3.notes: Segregation matrix documented. Finance and IT admin roles separated. Compensating controls logged for two legacy systems where full separation not feasible.

A.5.4.notes: Security accountability added to all manager KPIs in FY26. Annual team briefing requirement added to manager checklist.

A.5.5.notes: CERT-In, MEITY, and IRDA contacts documented. Reviewed after CERT-In 2022 directive. Next review: Jan 2027.

A.5.6.notes: Subscribed to CERT-In advisories, OWASP India chapter, and FS-ISAC. Monthly threat digest circulated to security team.

A.5.7.notes: Commercial TI feed (Recorded Future) under evaluation. Currently relying on CERT-In + OSINT. Formal TI register expected Q3 2026.

A.5.8.notes: Security gate added to Jira project template. Architecture review mandatory before sprint 1. DPIA triggered for projects with personal data.

A.5.9.notes: Asset register maintained in ServiceNow. 847 assets catalogued. Last full reconciliation: April 2026. Owners assigned for all Tier-1 assets.

A.5.10.notes: AUP covers devices, internet, email, AI tools, and social media. Acknowledged annually via HR system. 98% completion rate FY26.

A.5.11.notes: Offboarding checklist includes asset return sign-off. Integrated with HRMS termination workflow. IT access revoked same day for involuntary exits.

A.5.12.notes: Four-tier classification: Public / Internal / Confidential / Restricted. Classification decision tree distributed. Covers data at rest, in transit, and in use.

A.5.13.notes: Sensitivity labels deployed for M365. Physical document labelling procedure drafted, pending training rollout. Target: Q2 2026.

A.5.14.notes: TLS 1.2+ mandatory for all data in transit. Approved transfer tools list published. Consumer file-sharing blocked by DLP policy.

A.5.15.notes: Policy defines need-to-know, least privilege, and segregation requirements. Covers logical, physical, and remote access. Semi-annual privileged access reviews conducted.

A.5.16.notes: Automated provisioning via Okta integrated with HRMS. Deprovisioning SLA: 4 hours for voluntary, immediate for involuntary. No shared accounts policy enforced.

A.5.17.evidenceRef: POL-AUTH-001 Authentication Policy + JIRA-2341 MFA Rollout
A.5.17.notes: MFA enforced for all cloud apps and VPN via Okta. Password manager (1Password Teams) licensed for all staff. 14+ char passphrase minimum.

A.5.18.notes: Formal request/approval in ServiceNow. Quarterly privileged access reviews completed. Last review: May 2026 - 3 accounts revoked.

A.5.19.evidenceRef: VENDOR-001 Supplier Security Policy + Supplier Register
A.5.19.notes: Security clauses standard in all vendor contracts. 34 suppliers assessed. Tier-1 suppliers require ISO 27001 or SOC 2 evidence annually.

A.5.20.notes: Standard security schedule included in all vendor agreements. Covers data handling, incident notification (24h), audit rights, and data deletion at contract end.

A.5.21.notes: Critical software dependencies mapped. SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) process under implementation. AWS, Azure, and Salesforce assessed. Long-tail SaaS assessment pending.

A.5.22.notes: Annual security reviews for Tier-1 suppliers. Change notification process documented in vendor contracts. Q1 2026 review: all 8 Tier-1 suppliers reviewed.

A.5.23.evidenceRef: POL-CLOUD-001 Cloud Security Policy + Cloud Service Register
A.5.23.notes: Cloud policy covers data residency (India preferred), classification limits, and exit strategy. 23 approved cloud services registered. Shadow IT monitoring via CASB.

A.5.24.notes: IRP covers detection through post-incident review. IRT roles assigned. Tabletop exercise conducted March 2026. CERT-In 6-hour reporting obligation documented.

A.5.25.notes: 5-severity event classification matrix. Tier 1/2 events auto-escalated via SIEM. Average triage time: 18 minutes in Q1 2026.

A.5.26.notes: 12 incidents logged YTD 2026. Average containment time: 4.2 hours. All major incidents had post-incident reviews completed within 5 days.

A.5.27.notes: PIR mandatory for all P1/P2 incidents. Lessons learned fed into quarterly security review. 4 PIRs completed in 2026; all remediation actions tracked in Jira.

A.5.28.notes: Draft forensic evidence collection procedure under legal review. Immutable SIEM logs retained for 13 months. Chain of custody form drafted.

A.5.29.evidenceRef: BCP-001 Business Continuity Plan - Security Annex
A.5.29.notes: Security controls during disruption documented in BCP Annex B. Critical security controls designated as must-maintain. BCP tested annually.

A.5.30.notes: RTO/RPO defined for all Tier-1 systems. Multi-AZ deployment active for production. Full failover test scheduled September 2026.

A.5.31.notes: Register covers IT Act 2000, DPDP Act 2023, CERT-In directions, IRDAI cyber guidelines. Reviewed quarterly. DPO owns DPDP obligations.

A.5.32.notes: All 142 software titles licensed and tracked in ServiceNow SAM module. Annual audit completed April 2026. No unlicensed software found.

A.5.33.notes: Retention schedule covers all 14 record categories. Legal holds process documented. Disposal tracked with certificates of destruction.

A.5.34.evidenceRef: DPO-001 Privacy Program Charter + RoPA v2.1
A.5.34.notes: Full DPDP Act 2023 compliance program active. RoPA maintained. DPIAs completed for 6 high-risk processing activities. Consent mechanism deployed on all data collection forms.

A.5.35.notes: Annual internal ISMS audit completed April 2026 by external GRC consultant. 3 minor findings, all closed. Results presented to Board in May 2026.

A.5.36.notes: Manual compliance reviews conducted quarterly by department heads. Automated compliance monitoring via M365 Compliance Center being expanded. Target: automated dashboards by Q3 2026.

A.5.37.notes: 47 documented operating procedures in SharePoint. All procedures version-controlled. Last full review: March 2026. Owners assigned for each procedure.

A.6.1.notes: Background checks via AuthBridge for all permanent and contract hires. Identity, address, criminal, education, and employment verified. Records retained 7 years.

A.6.2.notes: Security obligations in all employment contracts. NDA signed before Day 1 access. DPDP Act obligations included in contract language since October 2023.

A.6.3.evidenceRef: TRAIN-001 Security Awareness Program + LMS Reports
A.6.3.notes: Annual mandatory security awareness via Knowbe4. 96% completion rate FY26. Phishing simulations: 4 per year. Failure rate down from 22% to 8% YoY.

A.6.4.notes: IS violation disciplinary process documented in employee handbook. Graduated process: verbal warning → written warning → termination. No IS-related disciplinary actions in FY26.

A.6.5.notes: Post-employment confidentiality obligations in all contracts. Exit interviews include security obligations reminder. NDA acknowledgement at exit.

A.6.6.notes: NDA required for all employees, contractors, and third parties with data access. NDA register maintained. Templates reviewed by legal annually.

A.6.7.notes: Remote working policy covers VPN mandatory, MDM-enrolled devices only, clean-desk home office requirements, and public Wi-Fi prohibition. 340 remote employees covered.

A.6.8.notes: Report to security@acmehealth.in or via ServiceNow portal. 2-hour reporting requirement. No-blame culture documented. 47 events reported in FY26 vs 12 in FY25 (cultural improvement).

A.7.1.notes: Three security zones defined: Public, Internal, Restricted. Server room and data centre classified Restricted. Access card system covers all zones.

A.7.2.notes: Biometric + card access for server room. Visitor log maintained at reception. Visitors escorted at all times in Restricted zones. Access reviewed quarterly.

A.7.3.notes: Quarterly physical walkthroughs. Locked cabinets for sensitive documents in HR and Finance. Clean-desk policy enforced. Last walkthrough: May 2026 - 2 minor findings resolved.

A.7.4.notes: 32 CCTV cameras covering all access points and server room. 60-day retention. Alarm system with 24/7 monitoring by external guard service. After-hours access logged.

A.7.5.notes: Threat assessment covers: flooding (low risk, Bengaluru office elevated), fire (suppression system installed), power (2 UPS units + DG backup). Last reviewed: January 2026.

A.7.6.notes: No personal mobile phones in server room. Lone-worker protocol for after-hours server room access. Access restricted to 4 named individuals.

A.7.7.evidenceRef: POL-CDS-001 Clear Desk and Screen Policy
A.7.7.notes: Policy published and acknowledged by all staff. Screen lock at 5 minutes enforced via GPO. Monthly clean-desk spot checks by Floor Security Officers.

A.7.8.notes: Server room temperature maintained at 20-24°C with humidity monitoring. Workstations in customer-facing areas screen-guarded. Cable management documentation updated Q1 2026.

A.7.9.evidenceRef: POL-MOB-001 Mobile Device Policy + MDM Console
A.7.9.notes: All laptops encrypted (BitLocker). Remote wipe capability via Intune. VPN mandatory for corporate access. Lost device SLA: 2 hours to report, immediate remote wipe.

A.7.10.notes: USB drives prohibited except IT-issued encrypted drives. Media register for backup tapes. End-of-life media shredded by certified vendor. Certificates retained.

A.7.11.notes: 2x UPS with 45-minute runtime. DG tested monthly. Temperature/humidity sensors with automated alerts. No utility-related downtime in FY26.

A.7.12.notes: All cables labelled and documented in Visio diagrams. Data and power cables separated in raised floor. Patch panels in locked cabinets.

A.7.13.notes: Maintenance schedule for 34 critical hardware assets. Only approved engineers permitted. Data sanitized before off-site maintenance. Last full maintenance cycle: February 2026.

A.7.14.evidenceRef: PHYS-010 Secure Disposal Procedure + Disposal Certs
A.7.14.notes: 3 certified vendors for hardware disposal. NIST 800-88 overwriting for HDDs, physical shredding for SSDs. 47 devices disposed FY26 with certificates on file.

A.8.1.evidenceRef: POL-EP-001 Endpoint Security Policy + Intune Compliance
A.8.1.notes: Full-disk encryption, EDR (CrowdStrike), MDM (Intune), OS patching SLA 14 days critical. 98.3% device compliance in May 2026. Non-compliant devices blocked from corp network.

A.8.2.notes: CyberArk PAM deployed for all privileged accounts. No standing admin rights. JIT access with 4-hour sessions. Session recording enabled for all server admin sessions.

A.8.3.evidenceRef: POL-AC-001 Access Control Policy + RBAC Matrix
A.8.3.notes: RBAC implemented in all core applications. Data access restricted by classification. Application security reviews include access control testing. Last review: April 2026.

A.8.4.notes: GitHub Enterprise with SSO. Branch protection on main. Required reviewers: minimum 2 for production changes. Trufflehog scanning in CI pipeline for secret detection.

A.8.5.notes: Okta enforces MFA for all 127 integrated applications. Legacy auth (Basic, NTLM) disabled. Phishing-resistant MFA (YubiKeys) for privileged accounts and executives.

A.8.6.notes: CloudWatch + Datadog for capacity monitoring. Alerts at 75% CPU/memory/storage. Quarterly capacity reviews. No capacity-related incidents in FY26.

A.8.7.notes: CrowdStrike deployed on 100% of managed endpoints. Email gateway (Proofpoint) scanning. Web proxy (Zscaler) filtering. EDR alert queue reviewed daily by SOC.

A.8.8.notes: Tenable.io weekly scans for internet-facing, monthly for internal. SLAs: Critical ≤7 days (met 94%), High ≤30 days (met 89%). 0 Critical open vulnerabilities as of June 2026.

A.8.9.notes: CIS Benchmark Level 1 for all server types. Terraform enforces cloud baselines. Drift detection via AWS Config. 99.1% baseline compliance in May 2026.

A.8.10.notes: Automated deletion at end of retention via DLP policy. Right to erasure requests (DPDP Act) handled within 72 hours. 8 erasure requests fulfilled in FY26. Deletion logs maintained.

A.8.11.notes: Production data prohibited in non-production environments. Masking via Informatica for all PII fields. Dev/QA environments audited quarterly for real data. Last audit: April 2026 - clean.

A.8.12.notes: Microsoft Purview DLP covering email, SharePoint, Teams, and endpoint. USB write blocked for non-approved devices. 23 DLP alerts in May 2026, 21 false positives tuned out.

A.8.13.notes: 3-2-1 backup: daily to local NAS, weekly to S3 (Mumbai region), monthly to Glacier. Restore tested quarterly. Last restore test: May 2026 - successful. RTO 4h, RPO 24h for Tier-1.

A.8.14.notes: All production workloads in multi-AZ (AWS ap-south-1). RDS Multi-AZ, ELB, Auto Scaling Groups. Failover tested biannually. Last test: March 2026 - 2.3 min failover.

A.8.15.notes: Splunk SIEM ingesting 4.2 billion events/day. 13-month online retention. Log sources: all AWS services, endpoints, network devices, applications. Tamper-evident via S3 write-once.

A.8.16.notes: 47 active SIEM detection rules. Alert tiers: P1 (15-min response), P2 (1-hour), P3 (next business day). Weekly alert review and tuning session. MTTD improved 34% YoY.

A.8.17.notes: AWS NTP (169.254.169.123) for cloud resources. On-prem NTP hierarchy from ISP stratum-1. Drift monitoring in Datadog. Alert threshold: >500ms.

A.8.18.notes: Approved utility list maintained in ServiceNow. Production use requires manager approval in ticket. All privileged tool usage logged in CyberArk. Quarterly review of approved list.

A.8.19.evidenceRef: OPS-SW-001 Software Installation Policy + Intune Allowlist
A.8.19.notes: Application allowlist enforced via Intune. Production software deployment via approved CI/CD pipeline only. Change management required for all production software changes.

A.8.20.evidenceRef: NET-001 Network Security Architecture + Firewall Rule Register
A.8.20.notes: 4 network zones: DMZ, Production, Corporate, Management. Palo Alto NGFWs at all boundaries. Deny-by-default. Firewall rule review conducted April 2026 - 23 obsolete rules removed.

A.8.21.evidenceRef: NET-002 ISP and Network Service Agreements
A.8.21.notes: Primary and secondary ISP links with failover. Security clauses in both agreements. ISP SOC 2 reports reviewed annually. Network SLA: 99.95% uptime (achieved 99.97% FY26).

A.8.22.notes: VLANs: Production (10.1.x.x), Corporate (10.2.x.x), Dev (10.3.x.x), Guest (192.168.x.x), Management (172.16.x.x). Pen test confirmed cross-segment isolation. Last test: February 2026.

A.8.23.evidenceRef: SEC-WF-001 Web Filtering Policy + Zscaler Console
A.8.23.notes: Zscaler Internet Access deployed for all 340 users. Malware, phishing, adult content, and shadow IT categories blocked. 12,400 threats blocked in May 2026.

A.8.24.notes: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 preferred (1.2 minimum) in transit. AWS KMS for key management. Annual crypto audit. MD5, SHA-1, DES prohibited. Certificate inventory maintained.

A.8.25.notes: Threat modeling mandatory for new services. SAST (Semgrep), DAST (OWASP ZAP), and SCA (Snyk) in CI/CD pipeline. Security gate: 0 critical findings before production deploy.

A.8.26.notes: OWASP Top 10 addressed in all web applications. Security requirements as user stories in Jira. Acceptance criteria include security tests. ASVS Level 2 target for patient-facing apps.

A.8.27.evidenceRef: ARCH-002 Secure Architecture Principles + ADR Library
A.8.27.notes: Zero-trust principles documented. Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) include security rationale. Security architecture review mandatory before design sign-off.

A.8.28.notes: Language-specific secure coding guides published for Python, Java, and TypeScript. Semgrep enforces rules in CI. Annual developer secure coding training via Secure Code Warrior.

A.8.29.evidenceRef: SDLC-004 Security Testing Standard + PenTest Reports
A.8.29.notes: Annual penetration testing by external vendor (Appsecco). DAST in staging CI/CD. Security acceptance criteria in definition of done. Last pen test: January 2026 - 2 High findings, both resolved.

A.8.30.notes: Security clauses in outsourcing contracts. Code review rights established. Background check requirement added to 3 of 4 vendors. SBOM requirement pending contract renewal for 1 vendor.

A.8.31.notes: Strictly separated Dev/Staging/Production AWS accounts. Different IAM roles per environment. Production access requires manager approval. IaC (Terraform) ensures consistent but isolated environments.

A.8.32.evidenceRef: OPS-CHG-001 Change Management Policy + ServiceNow CAB
A.8.32.notes: CAB meets weekly. All changes categorised: Standard/Normal/Emergency. Security impact assessment in change template. Emergency change retrospective within 72 hours. 234 changes in Q1 2026, 0 security incidents from changes.

A.8.33.notes: Real personal data prohibited in all test environments. Synthetic data generated via Faker library. DPO approval required for any exception. No exceptions in FY26.

A.8.34.notes: Audit scope and schedule agreed with system owners. Read-only audit access provisioned via dedicated audit account. Audit activities logged. Audit tools reviewed and approved before use.
